rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex clauses only from requirement intent and visible symbols, avoiding inferred or unstated bounds.
Place component-type assumptions in type-level annexes; use implementation annexes only for bindings after subcomponents.
generic_rules: Map requirement expressions to AGREE syntax using only explicitly declared ports or constants from the AADL model.
Preserve the exact numeric or Boolean domain of the requirement without type coercion or implicit inference.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE annexes.
Boolean comparisons must match the port's declared type—do not coerce integers to bool or vice versa.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not invent bounds (e.g., x >= 0) not explicitly stated in the requirement.
